Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who is an INSS retiree or pensioner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

cpf.description: Customer's CPF (Brazilian individual taxpayer registry number).

userId.description: The ID of the user who owns this customer record.

LoanProposal.title: LoanProposal
LoanProposal.description: Represents a loan proposal for a customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LoanProposal entity.

userId.description: The ID of the user who owns this proposal.

netAmount.description: Net amount released to the customer.

grossAmount.description: Gross amount (contract value) of the loan.

approvalDate.description: Date the loan proposal was approved.

bankInputDate.description: Date the loan proposal was entered into the bank's system.

customerPaymentDate.description: Date the customer received the loan payment.

status.description: Status of the proposal (e.g., Averaged, In Progress, Rejected, Awaiting Debt Balance).

Commission.title: Commission
Commission.description: Represents a commission received for a loan proposal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Commission entity.

pendingPayment.description: Indicates if the commission payment is pending.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: Name of the loan product (e.g., Margin, CLT Margin, Supplementary Withdrawal).

UserSettings.title: UserSettings
UserSettings.description: Stores user-specific settings for dropdowns and other configurations.

proposalStatuses.description: List of possible statuses for a proposal.

commissionStatuses.description: List of possible statuses for a commission.

definition.description: Stores customer profiles. All customer operations will be accessible to the user if `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores loan proposals associated with customers. Access is restricted to the user who owns the proposal (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`).

params.name: loanProposalId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan proposal.

definition.description: Holds commission data tied to specific loan proposals. Access is controlled based on the associated loan proposal's ownership.

params.name: commissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the commission.

definition.description: Contains product information, used for product categorization.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores user-specific settings. Access is restricted to the owner of the settings.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching their auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to manage customer and loan information for a bank correspondent application, prioritizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging.  It focuses on authorization independence by denormalizing customer information into loan proposals, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. The structure supports secure listing of data and maintains invariants for ownership and timestamps. This approach enhances security rule simplicity and allows for atomic operations.

*   **Customers Collection:** Stores customer profiles. No security rules are enforced here, as customer data is considered private and only accessible to the authenticated user who owns the data. All customer operations will be accessible to the user if `request.auth.uid == customerId`
*   **Products Collection:** Contains product information, used for product categorization.
*   **LoanProposals Collection:** Stores loan proposals associated with customers. Includes denormalized customer data (e.g., customerId) to ensure authorization independence. Access is restricted to the customer who owns the proposal (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerId`).
*   **Commissions Collection:** Holds commission data tied to specific loan proposals. Access is controlled based on the associated loan proposal's ownership. This simplifies commission access management, as the security rules only need to check the loan proposal's ownership.
*   **UserSettings Collection:** Stores user-specific application settings, like custom lists for dropdowns. This allows for per-user customization of the application experience.

This design supports the required QAPs (Queryable Access Patterns):

*   **Secure Listing:** The segregation of data into user-specific subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/loanProposals`) enables secure and efficient listing of data because rules can be based on the path.
*   **Authorization Independence:** Denormalizing authorization context (e.g., copying customerId to loanProposals) removes the need for `get()` calls in security rules, making them simpler, more robust, and facilitating atomic writes.
*   **No Custom Claims (DBAC):** Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid` and data stored in the database.
*   **Clarity of Intent:** The structure clearly separates different types of data and establishes ownership through explicit user-specific paths and fields.
